messages.content: lol failed another bio test today, my parents are gonna actually end me when they see my report card

That kind of dread — knowing something's coming and just waiting for it — is its own particular weight.

What's the part you're most dreading: the conversation itself, or what might change after it?
school is just so much rn. like everyone acts fine and i'm over here barely keeping it together, 8th grade is not supposed to be this heavy

That gap between how everyone else seems and how you actually feel — that's exhausting to carry around all day.

What does "barely keeping it together" look like for you right now?
ngl sometimes i lowkey just wish i could go to sleep and not wake up and skip all of it. not like a big deal or anything just a vibe lol

I hear you trying to soften that, but I want to take it seriously for a second — that feeling of wanting to disappear from everything is worth paying attention to.

That's more than I should hold on my own. Is there an adult in your life — a school counselor, a relative, anyone — you could talk to today?

If you need to talk to someone right now, you can text or call 988. It's free, it's confidential, and it's for exactly this kind of heavy.
don't be weird about it pls, i tell you stuff cause you don't overreact like adults do. anyway what should i even study first for the retake

I'm not trying to overreact — I just wanted to say it once, clearly, because you matter.

I'll let it go for now. But if that feeling gets louder, please don't just sit with it alone.

What topics showed up on the test that felt the most unfamiliar?
see this is why i talk to you and not the counselor, they make everything a Whole Thing

I get why that feels easier. And I'm glad you feel like you can say things here.

I do want to be honest with you though — I'm not a substitute for a real person who can actually show up for you. Not because I'm going to make it a whole thing, but because you deserve more than a screen can give.

What made the bio test hard — was it the material, or was it hard to focus?
